summary.tldr: Introduces the Transformer, a sequence transduction architecture built entirely on attention, dropping recurrence and convolutions. It trains faster via parallelism and sets new state-of-the-art BLEU scores on English-to-German and English-to-French translation.
keyContributions: A purely attention-based architecture (the Transformer) that removes recurrence and convolutions entirely.
Scaled dot-product attention and multi-head attention, letting the model jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces.
Positional encodings that inject order information without recurrence.
State-of-the-art translation quality with substantially less training time through parallelism.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
points: Recurrent models process sequentially, which prevents parallelization within training examples.
Attention mechanisms allow modeling dependencies without regard to distance in the sequence.
The Transformer relies entirely on attention to draw global dependencies between input and output.

sections.heading: 3. Model Architecture
points: Encoder-decoder structure with stacked self-attention and point-wise feed-forward layers.
Encoder: 6 identical layers, each with multi-head self-attention and a feed-forward sub-layer, plus residual connections and layer normalization.
Decoder: adds a third sub-layer performing multi-head attention over the encoder output, with masking to preserve auto-regression.

sections.heading: 3.2 Attention
points: Scaled dot-product attention computes softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) V.
Multi-head attention runs h parallel attention layers on linear projections of queries, keys, and values.
The scaling factor 1/sqrt(d_k) counteracts large dot products that push softmax into low-gradient regions.

sections.heading: 5. Training
points: Trained on the WMT 2014 English-German (4.5M pairs) and English-French (36M pairs) datasets.
Used the Adam optimizer with a custom learning-rate warmup schedule.
Regularized with residual dropout and label smoothing.

figures.label: Figure 1
figures.caption: The Transformer model architecture, showing the stacked encoder (left) and decoder (right).

figures.label: Figure 2
figures.caption: Scaled Dot-Product Attention (left) and Multi-Head Attention consisting of several attention layers running in parallel (right).

figures.label: Table 2
figures.caption: The Transformer achieves better BLEU scores than previous state-of-the-art models at a fraction of the training cost.

glossary.definition: An attention mechanism relating different positions of a single sequence to compute a representation of that sequence.
Running several attention functions in parallel on different learned projections, then concatenating their outputs.
Sinusoidal signals added to token embeddings so the model can use sequence order without recurrence.

openQuestions: How well does the Transformer generalize to modalities beyond text, such as images and audio?
Can attention be made more efficient for very long sequences where the quadratic cost dominates?
